title: SSC CGL 2026
meta_description: SSC CGL 2026 — exam pattern, syllabus, eligibility, dates, salary details. Complete guide for Combined Graduate Level Examination.

hero.one_liner: Combined Graduate Level Examination for Group B and Group C posts across central government ministries and departments

key_details.label: Education Required
key_details.value: Bachelor's degree from any recognized university

key_details.label: Age Limit (General)
key_details.value: 18–27 years (varies by post, up to 32 for some)

key_details.label: Age Relaxation
key_details.value: OBC: +3 years, SC/ST: +5 years, PwD: +10 years

key_details.label: Application Fee
key_details.value: ₹100 (Exempted for Women, SC, ST, and Ex-Servicemen)

key_details.label: Exam Language
key_details.value: English and Hindi (except English Comprehension section)

exam_pattern.description: This is a screening test. You need to clear the cutoff to appear for Tier 2. Tier 1 score is NOT counted in final merit.
exam_pattern.negative_marking: 0.50 marks deducted for each wrong answer

exam_pattern.description: This determines your final rank and post allocation. It has three sessions conducted on the same day plus a computer proficiency test.
exam_pattern.duration: Session 1: 1 hr, Session 2: 1 hr, Session 3: 15 min

preparation_tips: Start with NCERT books for Class 6–10 to build your foundation in Math, Science, History, and Geography. This covers 60% of the General Awareness syllabus.
Previous year papers are gold. SSC repeats question patterns every year. Solve at least 20 previous year papers for Tier 1 and Tier 2.
Speed is everything in Tier 1 — you have only 36 seconds per question. Practice with a timer from day one. Target 90% accuracy with 50-second-per-question speed, then push faster.
For English, read The Hindu or Indian Express editorial page daily. Note down new words. This single habit improves vocabulary, comprehension, and grammar simultaneously.
Don't ignore Computer Knowledge for Tier 2 Session 3. It's 60 easy marks — basics of MS Office, networking, internet, and shortcuts. Most students skip this and lose free marks.
Join a free test series (Testbook, Oliveboard, PracticeMock all offer free mocks). Take at least one full mock test every week from month two onwards.

faqs.question: What degree is needed for SSC CGL?
faqs.answer: Any Bachelor's degree from a recognized university or equivalent. You can apply in your final year too — you need the degree at the time of document verification, not at the time of applying. A few specific posts need special degrees: Statistical Investigator needs a degree in Statistics, and Compiler needs a degree in Economics/Statistics.

faqs.question: Is there negative marking?
faqs.answer: Yes. In Tier 1, 0.50 marks are deducted per wrong answer (questions carry 2 marks each, so penalty is 25%). In Tier 2, it varies: 1.0 mark for Math/Reasoning sections, 0.25 marks for English/GK sections.

faqs.question: Does Tier 1 score count in final merit?
faqs.answer: No. Tier 1 is only qualifying. You need to clear the cutoff to reach Tier 2. Your final rank and post allocation is based entirely on Tier 2 marks.

faqs.question: What is the expected salary after SSC CGL?
faqs.answer: It depends on the post you get. Tax Assistant (Level 4) starts at around ₹25,000 in-hand. Inspector posts (Level 7) start at ₹44,000+. The highest post, Assistant Audit Officer (Level 8), gives ₹52,000+ in-hand. Salary is higher in metro cities due to higher HRA and DA.

faqs.question: How many attempts are allowed for SSC CGL?
faqs.answer: There is no limit on the number of attempts. You can keep appearing for the exam every year as long as you meet the age criteria. The upper age limit is 27-32 years depending on the post, with relaxations for reserved categories.

faqs.question: Can I give the exam in Hindi?
faqs.answer: Yes. The entire exam (except the English Comprehension section) is available in both Hindi and English. You choose your language preference during registration.
